feat_fig_fighting_style.name: Fighter's Fighting Style
feat_fig_fighting_style.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take the same Fighting Style option more than once, even if you get to choose again.

style_archery.name: Archery
style_archery.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

style_blind_fighting.name: Blind Fighting
style_blind_fighting.description: You have blindsight with a range of 3 meters. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind total cover, even if you're blinded or in darkness. You can also see an invisible creature within that range unless it successfully hides from you.

style_defense.name: Defense
style_defense.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

style_dueling.name: Dueling
style_dueling.description: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

style_gwf.name: Great Weapon Fighting
style_gwf.description: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

style_interception.name: Interception
style_interception.description: When a creature you can see hits a target other than you within your shields or weapons range with an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by 1d10 + your proficiency bonus, to a minimum of 0 damage. You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial weapon to use this reaction.

style_protection.name: Protection
style_protection.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within your shields range, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a shield.

style_superior_technique.name: Superior Technique
style_superior_technique.description: You learn one maneuver of your choice from among those available to the Battle Master archetype. If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw, the save DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier of choice. You gain one superiority die, which is a d6, and regain it when you finish a short or long rest.

style_thrown.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
style_thrown.description: You can draw a weapon that has the thrown property as part of the attack you make with the weapon. When you hit with a ranged attack using a thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

style_twf.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
style_twf.description: When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.

style_unarmed.name: Unarmed Fighting
style_unarmed.description: Your unarmed strikes can deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier on a hit. If you aren't wielding any weapons or a shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8. At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

feat_fig_second_wind.name: Second Wind
feat_fig_second_wind.description: On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

feat_fig_action_surge.name: Action Surge
feat_fig_action_surge.description: On your turn, you can take one additional action, except the Magic action. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. Starting at level 17, you can use it twice before a rest but only once on a turn.

feat_fig_signature_maneuver.name: Signature Maneuver
feat_fig_signature_maneuver.description: Starting at 2nd level, you learn a maneuver fueled by a superiority die. Choose a maneuver from the maneuver list that you can use with a d6 as your superiority die. You can use this maneuver once per initiative. Maneuver save DC = 8 + Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + Proficiency Bonus.

feat_fig_martial_archetype.name: Martial Archetype
feat_fig_martial_archetype.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype from the list available that you strive to emulate in your combat styles and techniques. The archetype you choose grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

feat_fig_martial_versatility.name: Martial Versatility
feat_fig_martial_versatility.description: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to fighters. If you know any maneuvers from the Battle Master archetype, you can replace one maneuver you know with a different maneuver.

feat_fig_asi.name: Ability Score Improvement
feat_fig_asi.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat. You gain this feature again at levels 6, 8, 12, 14, 16 and 19.

feat_fig_extra_attack.name: Extra Attack
feat_fig_extra_attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 11th level in this class and to four when you reach 20th level in this class.

feat_fig_indomitable.name: Indomitable
feat_fig_indomitable.description: Beginning at 9th level, when you are asked to roll a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead of rolling the save by spending an available charge of this feature. You can use this feature twice between long rests starting at 13th level and three times between long rests starting at 17th level.

man_ambush.name: Ambush
man_ambush.description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an initiative roll, you can expend one superiority die and add the die to the roll, provided you aren't incapacitated.

man_bait_switch.name: Bait and Switch
man_bait_switch.description: When you're within 1.5 meters of a creature on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 1.5 meters of movement and the creature is willing and isn't incapacitated. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Roll the superiority die. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature of your choice gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.

man_brace.name: Brace
man_brace.description: When a creature you can see moves into the reach you have with the melee weapon you're wielding, you can use your reaction to expend one superiority die and make one attack against the creature, using that weapon. If the attack hits, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.

man_commanders_strike.name: Commander's Strike
man_commanders_strike.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of your attacks and use a bonus action to direct one of your companions to strike. Choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you and expend one superiority die. That creature can immediately use its reaction to make one weapon attack, adding the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

man_commanding_presence.name: Commanding Presence
man_commanding_presence.description: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation), Charisma (Performance), or Charisma (Persuasion) check, you can expend one superiority die and add it to the ability check.

man_disarming.name: Disarming Attack
man_disarming.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to disarm the target, forcing it to drop one item of your choice that it's holding. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it drops the object you choose. The object lands at its feet.

man_distracting_strike.name: Distracting Strike
man_distracting_strike.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to distract the creature, giving your allies an opening. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has advantage if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.

man_evasive_footwork.name: Evasive Footwork
man_evasive_footwork.description: When you move, you can expend one superiority die, rolling the die and adding the number rolled to your AC until you stop moving.

man_feinting_attack.name: Feinting Attack
man_feinting_attack.description: You can expend one superiority die and use a bonus action on your turn to feint, choosing one creature within 1.5 meters of you as your target. You have advantage on your next attack roll against that creature this turn. If that attack hits, add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

man_goading_attack.name: Goading Attack
man_goading_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to goad the target into attacking you. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on all attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

man_grappling_strike.name: Grappling Strike
man_grappling_strike.description: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and then try to grapple the target as a bonus action. Add the superiority die to your Strength (Athletics) check.

man_lunging_attack.name: Lunging Attack
man_lunging_attack.description: When you make a melee weapon attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die to increase your reach for that attack by 1.5 meters. If you hit, you add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

man_maneuvering_attack.name: Maneuvering Attack
man_maneuvering_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to maneuver one of your comrades into a more advantageous position. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and you choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its reaction to move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks from the target of your attack.

man_menacing_attack.name: Menacing Attack
man_menacing_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to frighten the target. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

man_parry.name: Parry
man_parry.description: When another creature damages you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction and expend one superiority die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your superiority die + your Dexterity modifier.

man_precision_attack.name: Precision Attack
man_precision_attack.description: When you make a weapon attack roll against a creature, you can expend one superiority die to add it to the roll. You can use this maneuver before or after making the attack roll, but before any effects of the attack are applied.

man_pushing_attack.name: Pushing Attack
man_pushing_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to drive the target back. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and if the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you push the target up to 4.5 meters away from you.

man_quick_toss.name: Quick Toss
man_quick_toss.description: As a bonus action, you can expend one superiority die and make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the thrown property. You can draw the weapon as part of making this attack. If you hit, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.

man_rally.name: Rally
man_rally.description: On your turn, you can use a bonus action and expend one superiority die to bolster the resolve of one of your companions. Choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature gains temporary hit points equal to the superiority die roll + your Charisma modifier.

man_riposte.name: Riposte
man_riposte.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction and expend one superiority die to make a melee weapon attack against the creature. If you hit, you add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

man_sweeping_attack.name: Sweeping Attack
man_sweeping_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. Choose another creature within 1.5 meters of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on your superiority die. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

man_tactical_assessment.name: Tactical Assessment
man_tactical_assessment.description: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation), Intelligence (History), or Wisdom (Insight) check, you can expend one superiority die and add the superiority die to the ability check.

man_trip.name: Trip Attack
man_trip.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to knock the target down. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and if the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you knock the target prone.

arcane_shot_banishing_arrow.name: Banishing Arrow
arcane_shot_banishing_arrow.description: You use abjuration magic to try to temporarily banish your target to a harmless location in the Feywild. The creature hit by the arrow must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished; while banished, its speed is 0 and it is incapacitated. At the end of its next turn, it reappears. At 18th level, the arrow also deals 2d6 force damage.

arcane_shot_beguiling_arrow.name: Beguiling Arrow
arcane_shot_beguiling_arrow.description: Your enchantment magic causes this arrow to temporarily beguile its target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and one ally within 30 feet of the target is chosen; the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by that ally until the start of your next turn. The psychic damage increases to 4d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_bursting_arrow.name: Bursting Arrow
arcane_shot_bursting_arrow.description: You imbue your arrow with force energy drawn from evocation magic. Immediately after the arrow hits, the target and all other creatures within 10 feet of it take 2d6 force damage each. The force damage increases to 4d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_enfeebling_arrow.name: Enfeebling Arrow
arcane_shot_enfeebling_arrow.description: You weave necromantic magic into your arrow. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 necrotic damage. It must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the damage dealt by its weapon attacks is halved until the start of your next turn. The necrotic damage increases to 4d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_grasping_arrow.name: Grasping Arrow
arcane_shot_grasping_arrow.description: Conjuration magic creates grasping, poisonous brambles around the target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 poison damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it takes 2d6 slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the brambles with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC. Otherwise, the brambles last for 1 minute or until you use this option again. The poison and slashing damage increase to 4d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_piercing_arrow.name: Piercing Arrow
arcane_shot_piercing_arrow.description: You use transmutation magic to give your arrow an ethereal quality. Instead of making an attack roll, the arrow shoots forward in a line 1 foot wide and 30 feet long, ignoring cover. Each creature in that line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra 1d6 piercing damage. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage. The extra piercing damage increases to 2d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_seeking_arrow.name: Seeking Arrow
arcane_shot_seeking_arrow.description: Using divination magic, you grant your arrow the ability to seek out a target. Instead of making an attack roll, choose one creature you have seen in the past minute. The arrow flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring half cover and three-quarters cover. If the target is within range and a path exists, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes damage as if hit by the arrow, plus an extra 1d6 force damage, and you learn its current location. The force damage increases to 2d6 at 18th level.

arcane_shot_shadow_arrow.name: Shadow Arrow
arcane_shot_shadow_arrow.description: You weave illusion magic into your arrow, causing it to occlude your foe's vision with shadows. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to see anything farther than 5 feet away until the start of your next turn. The psychic damage increases to 4d6 at 18th level.

feat_sub_arcane_archer_lore.name: Arcane Archer Lore
feat_sub_arcane_archer_lore.description: At 3rd level, you learn magical theory or some of the secrets of history. Choose proficiency in either Arcana or History, and choose to learn either the Prestidigitation or Thaumaturgy cantrip.

feat_sub_arcane_shot.name: Arcane Shot
feat_sub_arcane_shot.description: At 3rd level, you learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. You learn three Arcane Shot options of your choice. Once per turn when you fire an arrow from a shortbow or longbow as part of the Attack action, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that arrow. You have two uses, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. You gain an additional Arcane Shot option at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

feat_sub_magic_arrow.name: Magic Arrow
feat_sub_magic_arrow.description: Whenever you fire a nonmagical arrow from a shortbow or longbow, you can make it magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. The magic fades immediately after it hits or misses its target.

feat_sub_curving_shot.name: Curving Shot
feat_sub_curving_shot.description: When you make an attack roll with a magic arrow and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet of the original target.

feat_sub_additional_arcane_shot_option.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
feat_sub_additional_arcane_shot_option.description: You gain one additional Arcane Shot option of your choice at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level. Your Arcane Shot options improve when you become an 18th-level fighter.

feat_sub_ever_ready_shot.name: Ever-Ready Shot
feat_sub_ever_ready_shot.description: Starting at 15th level, if you roll initiative and have no uses of Arcane Shot remaining, you regain half of its uses, rounded up.

feat_sub_student_of_war.name: Student of War
feat_sub_student_of_war.description: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

feat_sub_combat_superiority.name: Combat Superiority
feat_sub_combat_superiority.description: At 3rd level, you learn maneuvers fueled by superiority dice. You learn three additional maneuvers of your choice, you have four superiority dice which are d6s, and you regain all expended superiority dice when you enter initiative. Maneuver save DC = 8 + Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + Proficiency Bonus.

feat_sub_additional_maneuvers.name: Additional Maneuvers
feat_sub_additional_maneuvers.description: At 7th, 10th, and 15th level, you learn two additional maneuvers of your choice. Each time you learn new maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

feat_sub_additional_superiority_die.name: Additional Superiority Die
feat_sub_additional_superiority_die.description: You gain another superiority die at 7th level, and one more at 15th level.

feat_sub_know_your_enemy.name: Know Your Enemy
feat_sub_know_your_enemy.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn whether it is your equal, superior, or inferior regarding two of the following: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, AC, current hit points, total class levels, or Fighter class levels.

feat_sub_improved_combat_superiority_d8.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d8)
feat_sub_improved_combat_superiority_d8.description: At 10th level, your superiority dice become d8s.

feat_sub_restless.name: Restless
feat_sub_restless.description: Once per initiative, when you use Action Surge or Second Wind, you gain a superiority die.

feat_sub_improved_combat_superiority_d10.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d10)
feat_sub_improved_combat_superiority_d10.description: At 18th level, your superiority dice become d10s.

feat_sub_cavalier_bonus_proficiency.name: Cavalier's Bonus Proficiency
feat_sub_cavalier_bonus_proficiency.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

feat_sub_born_to_the_saddle.name: Born to the Saddle
feat_sub_born_to_the_saddle.description: You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid falling off your mount. If you fall off your mount and descend no more than 3 meters, you can land on your feet if you're not incapacitated. Mounting or dismounting costs you only 1.5 meters of movement instead of half your speed.

feat_sub_unwavering_mark.name: Unwavering Mark
feat_sub_unwavering_mark.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can mark the creature until the end of your next turn. While it is within 1.5 meters of you, the marked creature has disadvantage on any attack roll that doesn't target you. If a marked creature deals damage to anyone other than you, you can make a special melee weapon attack against it as a bonus action on your next turn; you have advantage on the attack roll, and on a hit the weapon deals extra damage equal to half your fighter level. You can use this special attack a number of times equal to your Strength modifier, minimum once, and regain all expended uses on a long rest.

feat_sub_warding_maneuver.name: Warding Maneuver
feat_sub_warding_maneuver.description: At 7th level, when you or a creature you can see within 1.5 meters of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction if you're wielding a melee weapon or a shield. Roll 1d8 and add the number rolled to the target's AC against that attack; if the attack still hits, the target has resistance against the attack's damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, minimum once, and regain all expended uses on a long rest.

feat_sub_hold_the_line.name: Hold the Line
feat_sub_hold_the_line.description: When a creature enters your reach, you can use your reaction to make an opportunity attack against it. If you hit, the creature's speed becomes 0 for the rest of the turn.

feat_sub_ferocious_charger.name: Ferocious Charger
feat_sub_ferocious_charger.description: Starting at 15th level, if you move at least 3 meters in a straight line right before attacking a creature and you hit it with the attack, that target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

feat_sub_vigilant_defender.name: Vigilant Defender
feat_sub_vigilant_defender.description: Starting at 18th level, in combat you get a special reaction that you can take once on every creature's turn, except your turn. You can use this special reaction only to make an opportunity attack, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

feat_sub_improved_critical.name: Improved Critical
feat_sub_improved_critical.description: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

feat_sub_remarkable_athlete.name: Remarkable Athlete
feat_sub_remarkable_athlete.description: Starting at 7th level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded up, to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus. In addition, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

feat_sub_additional_fighting_style.name: Additional Fighting Style
feat_sub_additional_fighting_style.description: At 10th level, you gain one additional Fighting Style.

feat_sub_superior_critical.name: Superior Critical
feat_sub_superior_critical.description: Starting at 15th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

feat_sub_survivor.name: Survivor
feat_sub_survivor.description: At 18th level, at the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.

feat_sub_spellcasting.name: Eldritch Knight's Spellcasting
feat_sub_spellcasting.description: At 3rd level, you augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells. You know two cantrips from the arcanist spell list and you learn more as you level up. You know three 1st-level arcanist spells of your choice, two of which must be abjuration, evocation, or necromancy. You use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. You can use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your arcanist spells, and only while wielding the weapon created through your Weapon Bond feature do you have access to this spellcasting in your subclass setup.

feat_sub_weapon_bond.name: Weapon Bond
feat_sub_weapon_bond.description: At 3rd level, you learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. The weapon must be within your reach throughout the ritual, and at the end you forge the bond. Once bonded, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you are incapacitated. If it is on the same plane of existence, you can summon that weapon as a bonus action, causing it to teleport to your hand. You can have up to two bonded weapons.

feat_sub_war_magic.name: War Magic
feat_sub_war_magic.description: Beginning at 7th level, when you use your action to cast a cantrip, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

feat_sub_eldritch_strike.name: Eldritch Strike
feat_sub_eldritch_strike.description: At 10th level, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

feat_sub_arcane_charge.name: Arcane Charge
feat_sub_arcane_charge.description: At 15th level, you can teleport up to 9 meters to an unoccupied space you can see when you use your Action Surge. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

feat_sub_improved_war_magic.name: Improved War Magic
feat_sub_improved_war_magic.description: Starting at 18th level, when you use your action to cast a spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

feat_sub_knighthood.name: Knighthood
feat_sub_knighthood.description: The forces of Manyhal are organized in legions, each one a family of 500 members. The highest members of each legion are captains, commanders and knights. The lowest form of nobility gives way to honor, wealth, respect and prosperity.

feat_sub_rallying.name: Rallying
feat_sub_rallying.description: When you use your Second Wind feature, you can choose up to a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus within 18 meters of you that are your allies. Each one regains hit points equal to your fighter level + your Charisma modifier, provided that the creature can see or hear you. Additionally, once per turn when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to command one of your companions within your banner's range. That creature can immediately use its reaction to take an action or, instead, its movement.

feat_sub_knights_standard.name: Knight's Standard
feat_sub_knights_standard.description: As an action, you may plant the banner of your liege's house on the ground, bolstering your allies' morale. Allies within 9 meters of your banner have advantage against being frightened or disarmed.

feat_sub_royal_envoy.name: Royal Envoy
feat_sub_royal_envoy.description: At 7th level, you gain expertise with a Charisma skill with which you are already proficient. Any roll you make with a Charisma skill you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10. Additionally, any ally within your banner's range has advantage against being charmed.

feat_sub_legion_commander.name: Legion Commander
feat_sub_legion_commander.description: Starting at 15th level, your banner's range increases from 9 to 18 meters. Additionally, any creature within your banner's range gains +1 AC and 1.5 meters of movement.

feat_sub_uncheckable.name: Uncheckable
feat_sub_uncheckable.description: Starting at 18th level, when two allies or more are conscious and inside your banner's range, neither you nor them can be flanked. Additionally, you become immune to the Dazed, Grappled, Prone and Stunned conditions.

feat_sub_rune_carver.name: Rune Carver
feat_sub_rune_carver.description: You can use magic runes to enhance your gear. You learn two runes of your choice and, each time you gain a level in this class, you can replace one rune you know with a different one from this feature. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch a number of objects equal to the number of runes you know and inscribe a different rune onto each object. An object can bear only one rune at a time.

rune_cloud.name: Cloud Rune
rune_cloud.description: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks and Charisma (Deception) checks. When you or a creature you can see within 9 meters is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and choose a different creature within 9 meters of you, other than the attacker, to become the target of the attack using the same roll. Once you invoke this rune, it loses its power until you finish a short or long rest.

rune_fire.name: Fire Rune
rune_fire.description: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses your proficiency with a tool. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can invoke the rune to summon fiery shackles: the target takes an extra 2d6 fire damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute. While restrained, the target takes 2d6 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

rune_frost.name: Frost Rune
rune_frost.description: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks and Charisma (Intimidation) checks. As a bonus action, you can invoke the rune for 10 minutes to gain a +2 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Constitution. Once you invoke this rune, it loses its power until you finish a short or long rest.

rune_hill.name: Hill Rune
rune_hill.description: While wearing or carrying an object that bears this rune, you have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and you have resistance against poison damage. As a bonus action, you can invoke the rune to gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute. This rune requires 7th level or higher.

rune_stone.name: Stone Rune
rune_stone.description: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks and darkvision out to a range of 36 meters. When a creature you can see ends its turn within 9 meters of you, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is charmed by you for 1 minute, its speed is 0, and it is incapacitated.

rune_storm.name: Storm Rune
rune_storm.description: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks, and you can't be surprised as long as you aren't incapacitated. As a bonus action, you can enter a prophetic state for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated. Until the state ends, when you or another creature you can see within 9 meters makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can use your reaction to cause the roll to have advantage or disadvantage.

feat_sub_giants_might.name: Giant's Might
feat_sub_giants_might.description: As a bonus action, you magically gain benefits for 1 minute: if you are smaller than Large, you become Large; you have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws; once on each of your turns, one of your attacks with a weapon or an unarmed strike can deal an extra 1d6 damage on a hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

feat_sub_runic_shield.name: Runic Shield
feat_sub_runic_shield.description: You can invoke your rune magic to protect your allies. When another creature you can see within 9 meters is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to reroll the d20 and use the new roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses when you finish a long rest.

feat_sub_great_stature.name: Great Stature
feat_sub_great_stature.description: At 10th level, your rune magic permanently alters your body and your stature continues to grow with your power.

feat_sub_master_of_runes.name: Master of Runes
feat_sub_master_of_runes.description: You can invoke each rune you know from your Rune Carver feature twice rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

feat_sub_runic_juggernaut.name: Runic Juggernaut
feat_sub_runic_juggernaut.description: The extra damage you deal with Giant's Might increases to 1d10. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 1.5 meters.

classes.name: Fighter
classes.description: A master of martial combat, skilled with a variety of weapons and armor.

hp_details.at_higher_levels: 1d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per Fighter level after 1st

proficiencies_desc.armor: Light armor, medium armor, heavy armor, shields
proficiencies_desc.skills: Choose two from Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Athletics, History, Insight, Intimidation, Perception, or Survival

classes.quick_build: You can make a fighter quickly by following these suggestions. First, make Strength or Dexterity your highest ability score, depending on whether you want to focus on melee weapons or on archery (or finesse weapons). Your next-highest score should be Constitution, or Intelligence if you plan to adopt the Eldritch Knight martial archetype. Second, choose the soldier background.

subclasses.name: Arcane Archer
subclasses.description: A fighter who uses magic to empower ranged attacks.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: A tactical fighter who masters battlefield maneuvers.

subclasses.name: Cavalier
subclasses.description: A mounted protector and defender of allies.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: A martial specialist focused on physical perfection and critical hits.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: A fighter who blends martial prowess with arcane spellcasting.

subclasses.name: Legion Banneret
subclasses.description: A battlefield leader whose banner inspires and protects allies.

subclasses.name: Rune Knight
subclasses.description: A fighter who channels giant magic through runes and transformation.
